text.title: Lean Stack Bootstrapper Blueprint Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Lean Stack Bootstrapper Blueprint application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Stack has ≤5 services: Vercel (hosting + edge), Supabase (DB + auth + storage), Stripe (payments), Cloudflare (DNS + CDN + email forward), one observability (BetterStack / Sentry).
rule.rationale: ≤5 services keeps cognitive load + cost manageable; >5 = monitoring burden exceeds value.

rule.statement: Set a monthly cost ceiling ($50, $200, $500). Alert at 80%. Auto-pause non-essential at 100%.
rule.rationale: Lean stack autoscales; without ceiling, accidental run-up burns cash before next sale.

rule.statement: Do NOT introduce Docker, Kubernetes, or self-hosted services before the lean stack hits a real bottleneck. If unsure, you don't have one.
rule.rationale: Premature container/k8s is the #1 indie-hacker time sink; lean stack covers 90% of first-year needs.

rule.statement: Accept vendor lock-in as a feature. Free tier's job is shipping, not portability. Optimize portability after PMF.
rule.rationale: Portability obsession kills shipping cadence; pre-PMF, vendor lock-in is a feature.

rule.statement: Document the triggers to graduate: monthly cost > 30% of MRR, p95 latency > 1s, vendor outage > 4h/month. Otherwise stay lean.
rule.rationale: Explicit triggers prevent both premature scaling + denial of legitimate scaling needs.
